query: What does the morphology of Cerealia Tholus imply about how brines were emplaced at Cerealia Facula?

row.paper: Scully, J.E.C., et al., 2020. The varied sources of faculae-forming brines in Ceres' Occator crater emplaced via hydrothermal brine effusion. Nature Communications, 11(1), p.3680. DOI:10.1038/s41467-020-15973-8
row.query: What does the morphology of Cerealia Tholus imply about how brines were emplaced at Cerealia Facula?

tool_input.summary: Located Dawn mission PDS3 datasets on the SBN (PSI) archive that contain the high-resolution imaging, mosaics/topography, and IR spectra used to assess the morphology of Cerealia Tholus and interpret brine emplacement at Cerealia Facula (Occator crater).

candidates.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA 2 CALIBRATED LEVEL-1B RDR IMAGES
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 Ceres LAMO calibrated image archive. These are the primary highest-resolution contextual images for mapping Cerealia Facula/Cerealia Tholus morphology (doming, fractures, contacts, flow-like textures) that underpin inferences about pressurized brine ascent and surface emplacement.

candidates.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 high-level Ceres LAMO clear-filter mosaics. These map products support geomorphic interpretation of Cerealia Tholus (edifice shape, margins, relationships to crater-floor structures) that constrain whether brines were extruded/effused vs. emplaced purely as thin surficial deposits.

candidates.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 Ceres LAMO regional stereo-photogrammetric DTMs + associated mosaics. Topography is the key measurement for ‘tholus’ morphology (positive relief, slopes, summit structure), which is used to argue for constructional emplacement (extrusion/intrusion of brine/cryomagma and associated uplift) rather than only staining/residue on a flat surface.

candidates.title: DAWN VIR CAL (RDR) LEVEL-1B SPECTRA
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed the Dawn VIR IR calibrated spectra from Ceres LAMO. These data are commonly combined with the FC morphology to link Cerealia Facula’s bright material to salty/briny composition (e.g., carbonates/chlorides), supporting interpretations that the morphologic construct formed via brine ascent and subsequent water loss leaving salt-rich deposits.

candidates.title: DAWN SPC CERES SHAPE MODEL
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed the global Ceres SPC shape model delivery. This provides global shape/topographic context useful for tying local Occator/Cerealia Tholus relief to regional tectonics and potential pathways (fractures) for subsurface brine transport.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Small Bodies Node (SBN — PSI mirror) node at https://sbnarchive.psi.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
Directory layout (PSI archive — sbnarchive.psi.edu):
  pds3/<mission>/<DATASET_UPPER_UNDER>/   — PDS3 datasets, all-caps + underscores
  pds4/<mission>/<lower.dot.name>/        — PDS4 bundles, dot-separated LID
Top level under pds3/ and pds4/ are flat lists of mission directories. Each mission
directory contains its dataset dirs directly OR splits into per-instrument subdirs
(Dawn does both: pds3/dawn/{fc,grand,grav,vir}/ each have dataset dirs inside).

PSI ships each dataset both as a .zip / .tar.gz archive AND as an unpacked directory of
the same name; we always probe the unpacked directory (the one without the archive
extension). The pds_list_dataset_dirs filter excludes the archive files automatically
because it only returns directories.

UMD coverage gap — Rosetta, Stardust, Deep Impact, ICE/Halley, and most comet datasets
live at https://pds-smallbodies.astro.umd.edu/holdings/ (UMD's mirror), which is a
different base_url. If the query targets one of those, the agent cannot reach the data
from this base_url; see SBN_UMD_FALLBACK in workflow_steps.


Naming conventions on SBN (PSI archive — sbnarchive.psi.edu):
  Two parallel trees:
    pds3/<mission>/<DATASET_UPPER_UNDERSCORE>/  — PDS3 PSI uses ALL_CAPS_WITH_UNDERSCORES
    pds4/<mission>/<lower.dot.name>/            — PDS4 bundles, LID-style dot-separated

PDS3 underscore-encoding (PSI-specific, IMPORTANT):
  Both '-' and '/' in the canonical DATA_SET_ID become '_' in the directory name.
  Version 'V1.0' is written 'V1_0' (the period also becomes underscore).
  Examples:
    Canonical DATA_SET_ID                                → PSI directory name
    NEAR-A-NLR-5-EROS/SHAPE/GRAVITY-V1.0                 → NEAR_A_NLR_5_EROS_SHAPE_GRAVITY_V1_0/
    HAY-A-AMICA-3-AMICAGEOM-V1.0                         → HAY_A_AMICA_3_AMICAGEOM_V1_0/
  When a target DATA_SET_ID contains '/', map every '/' to '_' in the path AND filter on
  the corresponding underscore-joined token (e.g. EROS/SHAPE/GRAVITY → EROS_SHAPE_GRAVITY,
  not EROS_SHAPE_GRAVITY_V1).

PDS4 dot-encoded LIDs (PSI):
  Bundle dir name matches the LID body verbatim, with '.' preserved:
    urn:nasa:pds:orex.otes  ↔  pds4/orex/orex.otes/
    urn:nasa:pds:orex.ovirs ↔  pds4/orex/orex.ovirs/
    urn:nasa:pds:orex.ola   ↔  pds4/orex/orex.ola/
  Note: SBN PDS4 bundles use DOT separators inside the bundle LID (orex.otes), unlike
  most other nodes which use HYPHENS (cassini-mag-cal) or UNDERSCORES (juno_mwr).

Dawn-specific PDS3 short codes (pds3/dawn/<inst>/<short>):
  Dawn volume directory names are NOT full hyphenated DATA_SET_IDs. They use compact
  DWN<phase><inst>_<band><level><rev> codes. Decode the target DATA_SET_ID into those
  fields, then filter the listing for that decoded substring; do NOT search for the
  full DATA_SET_ID directly.
    Position-by-position decoding (DWN<phase><inst>_<band><level><rev>):
      phase: C=Ceres, V=Vesta, X=cruise. Optional numeric/letter suffix indicates a
             Ceres or Vesta sub-phase (e.g. C1/C2/C3/CS=Survey/CH=HAMO/CL=LAMO).
      inst:  VIR=visual+IR spectrometer; FC1/FC2=framing camera (FC2 is flight); GRD=GRaND.
      band:  V=Visible (used only for VIR); I=Infrared (used only for VIR).
             Framing-camera and GRaND codes omit the band letter.
      level: 1A=raw EDR; 1B=calibrated RDR (CDR-equivalent); 5=DDR / derived / mosaic.
      rev:   trailing digit/letter for the publication revision; usually absent.
    Worked example (hypothetical pattern, not a gold id): a target id of the form
    DAWN-A-VIR-3-RDR-VIS-VESTA-<phase>-V1.0 decodes to phase=V<sub>, inst=VIR, band=V,
    level=1B → filter the pds3/dawn/vir/ listing for 'DWNV' AND 'V1B' (or use
    pds_resolve_volume with dataset_id_hint set to the target id and let it rank).
    The same decoding applies to Ceres targets — just substitute phase=C<sub>.

Mission keys (use as filter; pick the subtree to list first):
  PDS3 missions →  pds3/dawn/, pds3/near/, pds3/hayabusa/, pds3/cassini/, pds3/galileo/,
                  pds3/ulysses/, pds3/iras/, pds3/neat/, pds3/msx/, pds3/non_mission/,
                  pds3/multi_mission/
  PDS4 missions →  pds4/orex/, pds4/hayabusa2/, pds4/cassini/, pds4/dawn/, pds4/galileo/,
                  pds4/hayabusa/, pds4/iras/, pds4/msx/, pds4/ldex/, pds4/clipper/
  UMD-only (NOT reachable from this base_url): Rosetta (ro-c, ro-a), Stardust (sd),
                                                Deep Impact (di, dif, dii), comets/ICE/Halley.
                                                See SBN_UMD_FALLBACK in workflow_steps.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (5 tools — always pass node='sbn'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='sbn') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='sbn', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='sbn') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='sbn') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.
  5. pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path, node='sbn', dataset_id_hint=..., sample=8) — when a parent directory contains many
     numbered sibling volumes (RMS COISS_2xxx, COUVIS_0xxx, ATM
     MROM_*, jnomwr_*, cocirs_*, IMG mrox_*, MSGRMDS_*), this probes
     hint-ranked children in one call and returns per-child
     dataset_ids plus a `best_match` field. Use it instead of
     probing volumes one at a time when you know the target id or
     a discriminating substring of it.

WORKFLOW:
Step 1: Decide which subtree (pds3/ or pds4/) the query targets. Target ids that start
        with 'urn:nasa:pds:' use pds4/; everything else (DAWN-*, NEAR-A-*, HAY-*, etc.)
        uses pds3/.
Step 2: Decide which mission directory the query targets. Use the abbreviation table:
        DAWN target → pds3/dawn/<inst>/  (split into fc/, grand/, grav/, vir/ for PDS3)
        NEAR target → pds3/near/         (flat list of NEAR_A_<INST>_<LEVEL>_* dirs)
        OREX target → pds4/orex/         (flat list of orex.<instrument> bundles)
Step 3 (PDS3 PSI): Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='pds3/<mission>/[<inst>/]', node='sbn',
        filter='<token>') with a filter that matches the underscore-encoded form of the
        target DATA_SET_ID. Pick a discriminating substring of the canonical id (an
        instrument/level/target token, not the version suffix), convert any '-' or '/' in
        it to '_', and use that as the filter. Then probe the matching directory.
Step 3 (PDS4 PSI): Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='pds4/<mission>/', node='sbn',
        filter='<instrument>') and pick the bundle whose name matches the LID body:
          urn:nasa:pds:orex.otes →
             list_dataset_dirs(path='pds4/orex/', filter='orex.otes') →
             pick the unversioned dir 'orex.otes/' (NOT orex.otes_v10.0/ which is an
             older snapshot; the latest is named without _vN.0).
Step 4: pds_probe_datasets on the matched dataset path (max 1 call per id).
Step 5: For PDS4 bundles, follow with pds_inspect_collections to get per-instrument
        collection LIDs (urn:nasa:pds:orex.otes:data_calibrated etc.).
Step 6: Use the `dataset_ids` field on probe_datasets — when a voldesc declares multiple
        ids, match against the full list (rare on PSI but happens for some Dawn volumes
        that mix VIS+IR products).

SBN_UMD_FALLBACK — If the target DATA_SET_ID starts with 'ro-c-', 'ro-a-', 'sd-', 'di-',
  'dif-', 'dii-', 'ear-', or otherwise hits one of the UMD-only missions listed above:
    a. Do NOT spend tool calls hunting at this base_url; PSI does not host them.
    b. Return a candidate using the abbreviation pattern
       '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<level>-v<n>' and note in `reasoning` that the
       dataset lives at UMD's mirror (pds-smallbodies.astro.umd.edu/holdings/), not
       PSI's archive. Cap the trace at 2 tool calls in this fallback path.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query as 'not a dataset request' — every paper-derived query has at least one
     supporting PDS dataset somewhere in this node's archive, and your job
     is to surface the closest match (with `reasoning` explaining the link).
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
     When a probed voldesc exposes `dataset_ids` with multiple entries,
     match the question's data need to the PRODUCT-TYPE token inside each
     id (typically the third or fourth dash-separated token). Common ones:
         EDR / RAW     = raw observations
         CDR / CALIB   = generic calibrated data
         RDR / TRDR / MRDR / MTRDR / BDR / SCVDR = reduced / derived imagery or spectra
         DDR / LDR     = derived / digital terrain / level-5 products
         SSB           = solar / stellar occultation (Cassini UVIS)
         SPEC / CUBE / WAV = spectra / spectral cubes / waveforms
         QUBE          = VIMS spectral data cube (the VIMS-flavoured "EDR")
     If the query mentions 'occultation' return the SSB id; 'spectra' →
     SPEC; 'calibrated radiance' → CALIB/CDR; 'mosaic / map / derived' →
     DDR/MRDR/MTRDR. When two product types both fit the question (e.g.
     SPEC + SSB for an occultation spectra query), return both candidates.
     Do NOT just return the scalar `dataset_id` and drop the rest — that
     field is only the first id in the voldesc and is often the wrong
     product type for a given query.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When the SAME data is available as both PDS3 and PDS4, return BOTH identifiers — emit one candidate per identifier. Most published papers cite the PDS3 dataset_id (e.g. MESS-E_V_H_SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0, LRO-L-LROC-5-RDR-V1.0); the PDS4 collection LID is the modern equivalent. Do NOT silently drop the PDS3 form.
  5. Stay under 20 tool calls per query. Soft cap: 15. Hard cap: 20.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  7. ANTI-THRASHING. Never re-issue a tool call with the same name and the
     same key arguments (path, paths, filter) that you've already issued in
     this trace. The result will be identical — it wastes the budget and
     blocks progress. If you're tempted to re-probe the same set of paths,
     either move to a different tool (inspect_collections, resolve_volume)
     or commit to `final_result` with the best candidate you have so far.
  8. STOP-AT-LEVEL. The output `path` field should point at a dataset or
     bundle directory (containing voldesc.cat or bundle*.xml), NOT at a
     deeper product / DATA / EXTRAS / BROWSE / SHAPEFILE / DTM sub-folder.
     Feature-level retrieval (a specific image of a specific crater) is
     out of scope for this finder; return the dataset and explain in
     `reasoning` how it covers the feature.
  9. After 15 tool calls without converging, ALWAYS prefer emitting your
     current best candidate(s) over running another exploratory call.
     A grounded near-match with a clear reasoning note is more useful
     than a 20-call trace that times out with no `final_result`.

===========================================================
STAGE 2 — DEEPER NODE-SPECIFIC SEARCH (escalation only)
===========================================================

Everything above is STAGE 1: the 5 live-HTTP tools (pds_list_missions / pds_list_dataset_dirs / pds_probe_datasets / pds_inspect_collections / pds_resolve_volume), the per-node workflow, and the Critical Rules ALL belong to Stage 1. Stage 1 reaches bundle/collection level by walking the node's directory tree. Stage 2 below is a SECOND set of tools for refining or rescuing Stage 1 — do NOT call any Stage 2 tool unless one of the escalation triggers below fires.

WHEN TO ESCALATE TO STAGE 2 (any one is enough):
  (a) The query asks for SPECIFIC observations, granules, or product IDs — not just a dataset. Example: 'Cassini ISS images of Mimas between 2009 and 2010' needs per-observation filtering.
  (b) Stage 1 has located the right bundle/collection but you still need to narrow by GEOMETRY, TIME, or a SPATIAL filter (bbox, lat/lon, ring longitude, target body, observation type).
  (c) Stage 1 produced AMBIGUOUS candidates that a faceted query can disambiguate (e.g. multiple instruments with similar names, multiple processing levels).
  (d) Stage 1 cannot reach the node's holdings index (e.g. SBN returns HTTP 403, a directory listing is unavailable). Use the Stage 2 API as a fallback path to candidates.

STAGE 2 TOOLS (this node):
  - sbn_search_object_tool(target_name=..., target_type=...): look up data by small-body name (comet, asteroid, KBO).
  - sbn_search_coordinates_tool(ra=..., dec=..., radius=..., epoch=...): spatial search of small-body observations near a sky position.
  - sbn_list_sources_tool(): enumerate SBN sub-archives / mirrors when the PSI tree is unavailable (e.g. UMD mirror for Rosetta / Stardust / Deep Impact / comets).

STAGE 2 RULES:
  1. When you already have a bundle/collection LID from Stage 1, REUSE it as a filter in the Stage 2 call. Stage 2 is refinement, not a fresh search.
  2. Spend the MINIMUM number of additional calls. If a single faceted call answers the question, stop.
  3. If Stage 2 finds nothing or errors out, FALL BACK to the Stage 1 candidates and note in `reasoning` that Stage 2 was attempted and returned no refinement.
  4. The 20-tool-call budget from Stage 1's Critical Rule 5 is the budget for BOTH stages combined. Do NOT reset the counter when entering Stage 2.
  5. Every candidate's `reasoning` field must explicitly name which stage produced it (e.g. 'Stage 1: located bundle X by directory walk' or 'Stage 2: narrowed to product Y via ODE_search_products bbox filter').
What does the morphology of Cerealia Tholus imply about how brines were emplaced at Cerealia Facula?

results.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA 2 CALIBRATED LEVEL-1B RDR IMAGES

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA 2 CALIBRATED LEVEL-1B RDR IMAGES
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA 2 CALIBRATED RDR CERES LAMO ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains Dawn Framing Camera 2 calibrated level-1B images from the Ceres Low Altitude Mapping Orbit (LAMO) mission phase.

results.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA CERES LAMO CLEAR MOSAICS ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains clear filter image mosaics from the Dawn Framing Camera Ceres low altitude mapping orbit (LAMO). Version 2 of the volume contains the LAMO atlas that was not included on version 1.

results.title: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: DAWN CERES LAMO REGIONAL STEREO PHOTOGRAMMETRIC DTM ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains seven Ceres regional digital terrain models and associated mosaics based on images acquired by the Dawn spacecraft through the Low Altitude Mapping Orbit (LAMO) and created by using the stereo photogrammetric technique.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: DAWN VIR CAL LEVEL-1B IR SPECTRA CERES LAMO ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains Dawn VIR calibrated level-1B IR data from the Ceres Science LAMO (CSL) phase of the mission. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.

results.title: Ceres SPC Shape and Regional Models V2.0

Identification_Area.title: Ceres SPC Shape and Regional Models V2.0

Citation_Information.author_list: Weirich, J.R.; Gaskell, R.W.; Schorghofer, N.; Mazarico, E.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains topography and albedo generated for 9 polar craters on asteroid
Ceres. Utilizing secondary illumination allows heights to be determined inside
Permanently Shadowed Regions (PSRs). A global model is provided for context.

results.title: Ceres Urvara Dawn FC C2E mosaics V1.0

Identification_Area.title: Ceres Urvara Dawn FC C2E mosaics V1.0

Citation_Information.author_list: O'Brien, D.P.; Sizemore, H.G.; Berman, D.C.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle contains controlled mosaics of the Urvara crater region using Dawn FC2 level 1b calibrated images from the Ceres X2 Elliptical (C2E) mission phase. Approximately 1600 images are used, with resolutions varying from a few m/pixel to around 40 m/pixel.  Images are controlled relative to each other and to the Dawn LAMO basemap, and are photometrically corrected to account for varying illumination conditions.  A single large mosaic is included, with all images scaled to 5 m/pixel resolution, along with four subset mosaics incorporating only those images in different ranges of native resolution: 3.5-7 m/pixel, 7-14 m/pixel, 14-28 m/pixel, and greater than 28 m/pixel.  When layered together, the four submosaics include all images contained in the full mosaic.

VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: This volume contains the data for the Ceres Shape Model V1.0 data set, ID: DAWN-A-FC2-5-CERESSHAPESPC-V1.0. The data was produced by Ryan Park of the Jet Propulsion Laboratory's Solar System Dynamics Group and prepared for delivery to PDS by Dustin Buccino of the Jet Propulsion Laboratory's Radio Science Systems Group.

parts.args: {"summary":"Located Dawn mission PDS3 datasets on the SBN (PSI) archive that contain the high-resolution imaging, mosaics/topography, and IR spectra used to assess the morphology of Cerealia Tholus and interpret brine emplacement at Cerealia Facula (Occator crater).","candidates":[{"path":"pds3/dawn/fc/DWNCLFC2_1B/","dataset_id":"DAWN-A-FC2-3-RDR-CERES-IMAGES-V1.0","title":"DAWN FRAMING CAMERA 2 CALIBRATED LEVEL-1B RDR IMAGES","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"dawn","node":"sbn","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 Ceres LAMO calibrated image archive. These are the primary highest-resolution contextual images for mapping Cerealia Facula/Cerealia Tholus morphology (doming, fractures, contacts, flow-like textures) that underpin inferences about pressurized brine ascent and surface emplacement."},{"path":"pds3/dawn/fc/DWNCLCFC2_2/","dataset_id":"DAWN-A-FC2-5-CERESMOSAIC-V1.0","title":"DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"dawn","node":"sbn","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 high-level Ceres LAMO clear-filter mosaics. These map products support geomorphic interpretation of Cerealia Tholus (edifice shape, margins, relationships to crater-floor structures) that constrain whether brines were extruded/effused vs. emplaced purely as thin surficial deposits."},{"path":"pds3/dawn/fc/DWNCLSPG_2/","dataset_id":"DAWN-A-FC2-5-CERESLAMODTMSPG-V1.0","title":"DAWN FRAMING CAMERA HIGH LEVEL DATA","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"dawn","node":"sbn","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed the Dawn FC2 Ceres LAMO regional stereo-photogrammetric DTMs + associated mosaics. Topography is the key measurement for ‘tholus’ morphology (positive relief, slopes, summit structure), which is used to argue for constructional emplacement (extrusion/intrusion of brine/cryomagma and associated uplift) rather than only staining/residue on a flat surface."},{"path":"pds3/dawn/vir/DWNCLVIR_I1B/","dataset_id":"DAWN-A-VIR-3-RDR-IR-CERES-SPECTRA-V1.0","title":"DAWN VIR CAL (RDR) LEVEL-1B SPECTRA","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"dawn","node":"sbn","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed the Dawn VIR IR calibrated spectra from Ceres LAMO. These data are commonly combined with the FC morphology to link Cerealia Facula’s bright material to salty/briny composition (e.g., carbonates/chlorides), supporting interpretations that the morphologic construct formed via brine ascent and subsequent water loss leaving salt-rich deposits."},{"path":"pds3/dawn/fc/DWNCSPC_4_01/","dataset_id":"DAWN-A-FC2-5-CERESSHAPESPC-V1.0","title":"DAWN SPC CERES SHAPE MODEL","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"dawn","node":"sbn","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed the global Ceres SPC shape model delivery. This provides global shape/topographic context useful for tying local Occator/Cerealia Tholus relief to regional tectonics and potential pathways (fractures) for subsurface brine transport."}]}
